old_claim.note: Enterprise Resource Planning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The published 1988 relational warehouse architecture predates the ERP category coined in 1990.

source_shape.source_locator: The 1988 IBM Systems Journal paper describes an integrated warehouse of company data based firmly in the relational database environment for end-user reporting and analysis.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The 1988 IBM paper directly specifies an integrated company-data warehouse grounded in a relational database environment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The primary architecture demonstrates relational data warehousing before ERP terminology existed and identifies relational databases, not ERP, as its technical base.

ontology_before: Relational data warehousing depended on a later enterprise-software category.
ontology_after: Relational data warehousing depends directly on relational database systems and remains independent of later ERP packages.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: relational_data_warehousing no longer directly depends on enterprise_resource_planning.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to warehouses implemented only as ERP reporting modules.
Evidence shows the 1988 IBM architecture was not a data-warehouse architecture.
short_reason: The published 1988 relational warehouse architecture predates the ERP category coined in 1990.
